title: 113. Path Sum II
approach:
Carried a list down the recursion to track the current path. 
Added node value going down, removed it coming back up. 
Saved the list at valid leaves where targetSum == 0.
insight:
Backtracking on trees — one shared list travels the whole 
recursion. Add before going into children, remove after 
both children return. This keeps the list always reflecting 
exactly the current root-to-node path.

Save with new ArrayList<>(arr) at valid leaves — never the 
reference directly, because the same list object keeps 
mutating as traversal continues.
mistakes:
1. Saved ans.add(arr) instead of ans.add(new ArrayList<>(arr)).
   arr is the same object throughout — by the time you read 
   the result it is empty. Always save a snapshot not a reference.

2. Earlier version passed root instead of root.left / root.right 
   in the recursive call — infinite recursion on the same node.
